description: 100% plant-based diet with no animal products, rich in whole foods, legumes, and diverse plant proteins

vgn_tofu_scramble.name: Tofu Scramble with Vegetables
vgn_tofu_scramble.description: Savory turmeric-spiced tofu scramble loaded with bell peppers, spinach, and nutritional yeast for a cheesy flavor
instructions: Press tofu for 10 minutes, then crumble into bite-sized pieces.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Sauté bell pepper for 3 minutes until slightly softened.
Add crumbled tofu, turmeric, garlic powder, and black salt. Cook for 5-6 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add spinach and cook until wilted, about 2 minutes.
Remove from heat, sprinkle with nutritional yeast, and season with salt and pepper.

vgn_overnight_oats.name: Peanut Butter Banana Overnight Oats
vgn_overnight_oats.description: Creamy overnight oats with peanut butter, banana, chia seeds, and a drizzle of maple syrup
instructions: Combine rolled oats, oat milk, chia seeds, and cinnamon in a jar or container.
Stir in peanut butter and maple syrup until evenly mixed.
Top with half the banana slices and press gently into the oat mixture.
Cover and refrigerate overnight or for at least 4 hours.
In the morning, top with remaining banana slices and an extra drizzle of maple syrup if desired.

vgn_acai_bowl.name: Acai Berry Smoothie Bowl
vgn_acai_bowl.description: Thick and vibrant acai bowl topped with granola, fresh berries, coconut flakes, and hemp seeds
instructions: Blend acai packets, frozen banana, frozen berries, and almond milk until thick and smooth. Add more milk only if needed — the consistency should be thicker than a smoothie.
Pour into a bowl and smooth the surface.
Arrange granola, hemp seeds, coconut flakes, and fresh blueberries on top in rows.
Serve immediately before it melts.

vgn_chickpea_pancakes.name: Savory Chickpea Flour Pancakes
vgn_chickpea_pancakes.description: Protein-packed chickpea flour pancakes with sun-dried tomatoes, fresh herbs, and a lemon-tahini drizzle
instructions: Whisk chickpea flour, water, onion powder, baking powder, salt, and pepper until smooth. Let batter rest 5 minutes.
Fold in sun-dried tomatoes and parsley.
Heat olive oil in a non-stick pan over medium heat. Pour 1/4 cup batter per pancake and cook 3 minutes per side until golden.
Mix tahini with lemon juice and a splash of water to make a drizzle.
Serve pancakes with tahini-lemon drizzle on top.

vgn_berry_smoothie.name: Triple Berry Protein Smoothie
vgn_berry_smoothie.description: Thick and creamy smoothie packed with mixed berries, silken tofu for protein, spinach, and almond butter
instructions: Add soy milk and silken tofu to a blender first.
Add frozen berries, spinach, almond butter, and maple syrup.
Blend on high for 60-90 seconds until completely smooth and creamy.
Pour into a glass and serve immediately.

vgn_avocado_toast.name: Loaded Avocado Toast with White Beans
vgn_avocado_toast.description: Sourdough toast topped with mashed avocado, seasoned white beans, cherry tomatoes, and everything bagel seasoning
ingredients: 1/2 cup canned white beans, drained and rinsed
instructions: Toast sourdough slices until golden and crisp.
Mash avocado with lemon juice, salt, and pepper in a bowl. Lightly mash the white beans separately with a fork, leaving some texture.
Spread mashed avocado on each toast, then top with mashed white beans.
Arrange cherry tomato halves on top and sprinkle with everything bagel seasoning and red pepper flakes.

vgn_granola_bowl.name: Coconut Yogurt Granola Bowl
vgn_granola_bowl.description: Thick coconut yogurt layered with crunchy homemade granola, fresh mango, passion fruit, and toasted almonds
instructions: Spoon coconut yogurt into a bowl.
Top with granola on one side and fresh mango on the other.
Scoop passion fruit pulp over the top.
Scatter toasted almonds and pumpkin seeds over everything and drizzle with maple syrup.

vgn_sweet_potato_hash.name: Sweet Potato Breakfast Hash
vgn_sweet_potato_hash.description: Crispy sweet potato cubes with black beans, corn, peppers, and smoked paprika, served with sliced avocado
ingredients: 1 large sweet potato, peeled and diced into 1cm cubes
1/2 cup canned black beans, drained and rinsed
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add sweet potato cubes and cook for 12-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until golden and tender.
Add red onion and bell pepper. Cook for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Stir in black beans, corn, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper. Cook 2-3 more minutes until heated through.
Transfer to a plate and top with sliced avocado.

vgn_banana_walnut_oatmeal.name: Banana Walnut Oatmeal
vgn_banana_walnut_oatmeal.description: Warm and hearty oatmeal with caramelized banana, toasted walnuts, cinnamon, and a swirl of almond butter
instructions: Combine oats, oat milk, cinnamon, and a pinch of salt in a saucepan. Bring to a simmer over medium heat.
Cook for 5-6 minutes, stirring occasionally, until oats are creamy and thick.
While oats cook, slice banana in half lengthwise. Toast walnuts in a dry pan for 2 minutes.
Pour oatmeal into a bowl. Top with banana, toasted walnuts, a swirl of almond butter, and a drizzle of maple syrup.

vgn_chia_pudding.name: Mango Coconut Chia Pudding
vgn_chia_pudding.description: Luxurious chia pudding made with coconut milk and topped with fresh mango, toasted coconut, and lime zest
instructions: Mix chia seeds, coconut milk, maple syrup, and vanilla extract in a jar. Stir well to prevent clumping.
Refrigerate for at least 4 hours or overnight, stirring once after 30 minutes.
When ready to serve, stir the pudding and spoon into a bowl.
Top with diced mango, toasted coconut flakes, and fresh lime zest.

vgn_blueberry_muffin_oats.name: Blueberry Muffin Baked Oats
vgn_blueberry_muffin_oats.description: Baked oatmeal that tastes like a blueberry muffin, made with flax eggs, oat milk, and bursting with fresh blueberries
ingredients: 1 tbsp ground flaxseed mixed with 3 tbsp water (flax egg)
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Prepare flax egg by mixing ground flaxseed with water; let sit 5 minutes.
Combine oats, baking powder, cinnamon, and salt in a bowl. In a separate bowl, mix flax egg, oat milk, maple syrup, and vanilla.
Pour wet ingredients into dry and stir to combine. Gently fold in most of the blueberries.
Pour into a greased ramekin or small baking dish. Top with remaining blueberries.
Bake for 22-25 minutes until golden and set in the center. Let cool 5 minutes before eating.

vgn_tempeh_breakfast_wrap.name: Smoky Tempeh Breakfast Wrap
vgn_tempeh_breakfast_wrap.description: Warm whole wheat tortilla filled with crispy smoky tempeh, sautéed kale, avocado, and sriracha mayo
instructions: Marinate tempeh slices in soy sauce and smoked paprika for 5 minutes.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Cook tempeh for 3-4 minutes per side until crispy and golden.
In the same pan, quickly sauté kale for 2 minutes until wilted.
Mix vegan mayo with sriracha and spread on the tortilla.
Layer tempeh, kale, and avocado slices on the tortilla. Fold and serve.

vgn_chickpea_buddha_bowl.name: Roasted Chickpea Buddha Bowl
vgn_chickpea_buddha_bowl.description: Crispy roasted chickpeas over quinoa with roasted sweet potato, massaged kale, pickled onion, and tahini dressing
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Toss chickpeas and sweet potato cubes with olive oil, cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper. Spread on a baking sheet.
Roast for 25-30 minutes, tossing halfway, until chickpeas are crispy and sweet potato is tender.
Meanwhile, massage kale with a pinch of salt and half the lemon juice until softened. Quick-pickle onion slices in apple cider vinegar.
Whisk tahini with remaining lemon juice and 2 tbsp water to make dressing.
Assemble bowl with quinoa as the base, topped with kale, roasted chickpeas, sweet potato, and pickled onion. Drizzle with tahini dressing.

vgn_lentil_soup.name: Red Lentil Coconut Soup
vgn_lentil_soup.description: Warming red lentil soup with coconut milk, ginger, turmeric, and a squeeze of lime, served with crusty bread
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Sauté onion and carrot for 5 minutes until softened.
Add garlic, ginger, turmeric, and cumin. Cook 1 minute until fragrant.
Add red lentils, coconut milk, and vegetable broth. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer.
Cook for 18-20 minutes until lentils are completely soft and broken down.
Stir in lime juice, season with salt and pepper. Serve garnished with fresh cilantro.

vgn_black_bean_tacos.name: Spicy Black Bean Tacos
vgn_black_bean_tacos.description: Seasoned black beans in corn tortillas with mango salsa, pickled jalapeños, cashew crema, and fresh cilantro
ingredients: 1 can (400g) black beans, drained and rinsed
2 tbsp raw cashews, soaked and blended with 2 tbsp water and 1 tsp lime juice
instructions: Heat olive oil in a skillet. Add black beans, cumin, chili powder, and salt. Cook 5-7 minutes, mashing some beans for texture.
Make mango salsa by combining diced mango, half the red onion, a squeeze of lime, and chopped cilantro.
Blend soaked cashews with water and lime juice until smooth for the crema.
Warm corn tortillas in a dry pan for 30 seconds per side.
Fill tortillas with black beans, top with mango salsa, jalapeño slices, cashew crema, and remaining cilantro.

vgn_pad_thai.name: Tofu Pad Thai
vgn_pad_thai.description: Classic pad Thai with crispy tofu, rice noodles, bean sprouts, crushed peanuts, and tangy tamarind sauce
instructions: Soak rice noodles in hot water for 8-10 minutes until pliable. Drain and set aside.
Mix tamarind paste, soy sauce, maple syrup, and rice vinegar for the sauce.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a wok over high heat. Fry tofu cubes for 5-6 minutes until golden on all sides. Remove and set aside.
Add remaining oil to the wok. Sauté garlic for 30 seconds, add drained noodles and sauce. Toss for 2-3 minutes.
Add tofu and bean sprouts, toss to combine. Serve topped with crushed peanuts, green onions, cilantro, and lime wedges.

vgn_mediterranean_wrap.name: Mediterranean Hummus Wrap
vgn_mediterranean_wrap.description: Whole wheat wrap packed with creamy hummus, falafel, cucumber, tomato, Kalamata olives, and a drizzle of herb oil
instructions: If falafel are cold, warm them in the oven at 350°F for 8 minutes or microwave for 1 minute.
Spread hummus generously down the center of the tortilla.
Layer cucumber, tomato slices, olives, and falafel on top of the hummus.
Drizzle with olive oil mixed with oregano, sprinkle with parsley, salt, and pepper.
Fold the sides in and roll tightly into a wrap. Cut in half diagonally.

vgn_teriyaki_tempeh_bowl.name: Teriyaki Tempeh Rice Bowl
vgn_teriyaki_tempeh_bowl.description: Glazed teriyaki tempeh over brown rice with steamed broccoli, edamame, pickled ginger, and sesame seeds
instructions: Mix soy sauce, maple syrup, rice vinegar, sesame oil, garlic, and ginger for the teriyaki sauce.
Steam broccoli and edamame for 4-5 minutes until bright green and tender-crisp.
Heat vegetable oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Cook tempeh strips for 3-4 minutes per side until browned.
Pour teriyaki sauce over tempeh and cook 2 more minutes until glazed and caramelized.
Serve brown rice in a bowl, topped with teriyaki tempeh, steamed broccoli, edamame, sesame seeds, and pickled ginger.

vgn_quinoa_stuffed_peppers.name: Quinoa-Stuffed Bell Peppers
vgn_quinoa_stuffed_peppers.description: Colorful bell peppers stuffed with seasoned quinoa, black beans, corn, tomatoes, and topped with avocado crema
ingredients: 2 large bell peppers, halved and seeded
1/2 cup black beans, drained and rinsed
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Place bell pepper halves cut-side up in a baking dish.
Mix quinoa, black beans, corn, diced tomatoes, cumin, chili powder, 1 tbsp nutritional yeast, salt, and pepper in a bowl.
Stuff each pepper half generously with the quinoa mixture. Sprinkle remaining nutritional yeast on top.
Cover with foil and bake for 25 minutes. Remove foil and bake 5 more minutes.
Blend avocado with lime juice, a pinch of salt, and 1 tbsp water for the crema. Drizzle over stuffed peppers before serving.

vgn_thai_peanut_noodles.name: Thai Peanut Noodle Salad
vgn_thai_peanut_noodles.description: Chilled rice noodles tossed in a creamy peanut-lime dressing with shredded cabbage, carrots, edamame, and fresh mint
instructions: Cook rice noodles according to package directions. Drain, rinse with cold water, and set aside.
Whisk peanut butter, soy sauce, lime juice, rice vinegar, sriracha, and maple syrup until smooth. Add 1-2 tbsp warm water to thin.
Toss cooled noodles with peanut sauce until evenly coated.
Add shredded cabbage, julienned carrot, and edamame. Toss to combine.
Serve topped with chopped peanuts, fresh mint, and cilantro.

vgn_mushroom_risotto.name: Creamy Mushroom Risotto
vgn_mushroom_risotto.description: Rich and creamy risotto with mixed wild mushrooms, white wine, nutritional yeast, and fresh thyme
instructions: Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large pan. Sauté mushrooms with thyme for 5-6 minutes until golden. Remove and set aside.
In the same pan, heat remaining olive oil. Sauté onion for 3 minutes, add garlic and cook 1 minute more.
Add arborio rice and stir for 1-2 minutes until edges are translucent. Pour in white wine and stir until absorbed.
Add warm broth one ladle at a time, stirring frequently and waiting until each addition is absorbed before adding the next. This takes about 18-20 minutes.
Stir in nutritional yeast and sautéed mushrooms. Season with salt and pepper. The risotto should be creamy and flowing. Serve immediately.

vgn_cauliflower_tikka.name: Cauliflower Tikka Masala
vgn_cauliflower_tikka.description: Roasted cauliflower in a rich, spiced tomato-coconut sauce served over basmati rice with fresh cilantro and naan
ingredients: 1 large head cauliflower, cut into florets
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Toss cauliflower florets with 1 tbsp olive oil, 1 tbsp tikka masala spice, and salt. Roast for 20-25 minutes until charred at edges.
Meanwhile, heat remaining olive oil in a large pan. Sauté onion for 5 minutes, add garlic and ginger, cook 1 minute.
Add remaining tikka masala spice and stir for 30 seconds. Pour in crushed tomatoes and coconut milk. Simmer for 15 minutes.
Add roasted cauliflower to the sauce and simmer 5 more minutes to meld flavors.
Serve over basmati rice, garnished with fresh cilantro.

vgn_black_bean_burger.name: Smoky Black Bean Burger
vgn_black_bean_burger.description: Hearty black bean patty with oats and smoked paprika on a toasted bun with avocado, lettuce, tomato, and chipotle mayo
ingredients: 1 can (400g) black beans, drained and rinsed
2 tbsp ground flaxseed mixed with 3 tbsp water
1 tbsp vegan mayo mixed with 1/2 tsp chipotle in adobo
instructions: Prepare flax egg by mixing ground flaxseed with water; let sit 5 minutes. Mash black beans in a bowl, leaving some chunks for texture.
Add oats, onion, garlic, smoked paprika, cumin, flax egg, salt, and pepper. Mix until combined. Form into 2 patties.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Cook patties for 5-6 minutes per side until a firm crust forms.
Toast burger bun and spread chipotle mayo on both halves.
Layer patty, avocado slices, lettuce, and tomato on the bun.

vgn_miso_ramen.name: Veggie Miso Ramen
vgn_miso_ramen.description: Umami-rich miso broth with ramen noodles, marinated tofu, bok choy, corn, nori, and a soft swirl of chili oil
instructions: Marinate tofu slices in soy sauce for 10 minutes. Pan-fry in sesame oil for 3 minutes per side until golden.
Heat vegetable broth in a pot. Add garlic and ginger, simmer for 5 minutes.
Dissolve miso paste in a small amount of warm broth, then stir back into the pot. Do not boil after adding miso.
Cook ramen noodles according to package directions. Add bok choy to the broth for the last 2 minutes of simmering.
Divide noodles between bowls. Ladle miso broth and bok choy over noodles. Top with tofu, corn, nori strips, green onions, and a drizzle of chili oil.

vgn_greek_salad_bowl.name: Greek-Inspired Power Bowl
vgn_greek_salad_bowl.description: Marinated chickpeas, cucumber, tomatoes, Kalamata olives, and tofu feta over orzo with lemon-herb dressing
ingredients: 1 cup canned chickpeas, drained and rinsed
instructions: Marinate tofu cubes in apple cider vinegar, 1 tbsp lemon juice, garlic powder, oregano, and a pinch of salt for at least 15 minutes to mimic feta.
Cook orzo according to package directions. Drain, rinse with cold water, and toss with a drizzle of olive oil.
Season chickpeas with a pinch of oregano, salt, and pepper.
Make dressing by whisking remaining olive oil, lemon juice, oregano, salt, and pepper.
Assemble bowl with orzo, top with chickpeas, cucumber, tomatoes, olives, red onion, and marinated tofu. Drizzle with dressing.

vgn_sweet_potato_curry.name: Thai Sweet Potato Curry
vgn_sweet_potato_curry.description: Creamy Thai-style curry with sweet potato, chickpeas, spinach, and red curry paste in coconut milk over jasmine rice
ingredients: 1 large sweet potato, peeled and cubed
2 tbsp red curry paste (check vegan — no shrimp paste)
instructions: Heat coconut oil in a large pot over medium heat. Sauté garlic and ginger for 1 minute.
Add red curry paste and stir for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Pour in coconut milk, add sweet potato cubes, and bring to a simmer. Cook for 15 minutes until sweet potato is tender.
Add chickpeas, soy sauce, and maple syrup. Simmer 5 more minutes. Stir in spinach until wilted.
Serve over jasmine rice, garnished with fresh basil leaves.

vgn_seitan_stir_fry.name: Seitan and Vegetable Stir-Fry
vgn_seitan_stir_fry.description: Tender seitan strips stir-fried with snap peas, bell peppers, and mushrooms in a savory ginger-garlic sauce over rice
instructions: Mix soy sauce, hoisin sauce, and rice vinegar for the stir-fry sauce.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a wok over high heat. Stir-fry seitan strips for 3-4 minutes until browned. Remove and set aside.
Add remaining oil. Stir-fry garlic and ginger for 30 seconds, then add bell pepper, snap peas, and mushrooms. Cook 3-4 minutes until tender-crisp.
Return seitan to the wok, pour in sauce, and toss everything together for 1-2 minutes.
Serve over brown rice, topped with sesame seeds and sliced green onion.

vgn_lentil_bolognese.name: Lentil Bolognese with Spaghetti
vgn_lentil_bolognese.description: Rich and hearty lentil-based Bolognese sauce with mushrooms and walnuts, served over whole wheat spaghetti
ingredients: 1 cup brown or green lentils, cooked
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot. Sauté onion, carrot, and celery for 5 minutes until softened.
Add garlic, mushrooms, and walnuts. Cook 4-5 minutes until mushrooms release their liquid.
Stir in tomato paste, oregano, and dried basil. Cook 1 minute.
Add crushed tomatoes and cooked lentils. Simmer for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until thick and rich.
Cook spaghetti according to package directions. Serve sauce over spaghetti, garnished with fresh basil.

vgn_falafel_bowl.name: Baked Falafel Bowl
vgn_falafel_bowl.description: Crispy baked falafel over a bed of tabbouleh with hummus, pickled turnips, and garlic-tahini sauce
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Pulse chickpeas, parsley, cilantro, garlic, cumin, coriander, chickpea flour, salt, and pepper in a food processor until a coarse mixture forms (not a paste).
Form mixture into 8-10 small balls. Place on an oiled baking sheet and brush tops with olive oil.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, flipping halfway, until golden and crispy.
Make tabbouleh by mixing cooked bulgur with diced tomato, cucumber, lemon juice, remaining parsley, olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Assemble bowl with tabbouleh, baked falafel, hummus, and tahini drizzle.

vgn_jackfruit_tacos.name: BBQ Jackfruit Tacos
vgn_jackfruit_tacos.description: Pulled BBQ jackfruit in corn tortillas with tangy coleslaw, pickled red onion, and creamy avocado
ingredients: 1 can (400g) young green jackfruit in brine, drained and shredded
1/4 red onion, thinly sliced and pickled
instructions: Drain and rinse jackfruit. Shred with two forks to create a pulled texture.
Heat olive oil in a skillet. Cook jackfruit with smoked paprika for 5 minutes, then add BBQ sauce and 2 tbsp water. Simmer 10-15 minutes until saucy and caramelized.
Make quick coleslaw by tossing cabbage with apple cider vinegar, a pinch of salt, and pepper.
Warm corn tortillas in a dry pan.
Fill tortillas with BBQ jackfruit, top with coleslaw, pickled red onion, and avocado slices.

vgn_coconut_daal.name: Coconut Dal with Naan
vgn_coconut_daal.description: Comforting yellow dal with coconut milk, warming spices, and a tarka topping of mustard seeds and curry leaves
instructions: Cook lentils with water and turmeric in a pot, simmering for 20 minutes until soft and broken down.
Add coconut milk and diced tomato. Simmer 5 more minutes, mashing lentils slightly for a creamy texture. Season with salt.
For the tarka, heat coconut oil in a small pan. Add cumin seeds and mustard seeds. When they start to pop, add curry leaves and garlic. Cook 30 seconds.
Pour the tarka over the dal and stir to combine.
Warm the naan and serve alongside the dal, garnished with fresh cilantro.

vgn_burrito_bowl.name: Vegan Burrito Bowl
vgn_burrito_bowl.description: Loaded burrito bowl with cilantro-lime rice, seasoned black beans, roasted corn, pico de gallo, guacamole, and cashew crema
ingredients: 1/2 avocado, mashed with lime and salt
2 tbsp cashew crema (blended cashews, water, lime)
instructions: Stir lime juice and cilantro into cooked rice for cilantro-lime rice.
Heat olive oil in a skillet. Season black beans with cumin and chili powder, cook 5 minutes. In the same pan, char corn kernels for 3-4 minutes.
Make pico de gallo by combining diced tomato, red onion, cilantro, a squeeze of lime, and salt.
Mash avocado with lime juice and salt for guacamole.
Assemble bowl: lettuce base, cilantro-lime rice, black beans, charred corn, pico de gallo, guacamole, and cashew crema drizzle.

vgn_stuffed_sweet_potato.name: Loaded Stuffed Sweet Potato
vgn_stuffed_sweet_potato.description: Baked sweet potato stuffed with spiced lentils, sautéed greens, tahini dressing, and toasted pepitas
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Pierce sweet potato several times with a fork. Bake for 40-45 minutes until soft throughout.
Season cooked lentils with cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper. Warm in a small saucepan.
Sauté greens in olive oil with a pinch of salt until wilted, about 2-3 minutes.
Whisk tahini with lemon juice and 1-2 tbsp water for a pourable dressing.
Split sweet potato open, stuff with lentils and greens. Drizzle with tahini dressing and top with toasted pepitas.

vgn_mushroom_stroganoff.name: Mushroom Stroganoff
vgn_mushroom_stroganoff.description: Tender sliced mushrooms in a rich cashew cream sauce with smoked paprika, served over egg-free pappardelle
instructions: Blend soaked cashews with vegetable broth until completely smooth to make cashew cream.
Cook pasta according to package directions. Drain and set aside.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet. Sauté onion for 3 minutes, add mushrooms, and cook 6-7 minutes until deeply browned.
Add garlic, cook 1 minute. Stir in soy sauce, smoked paprika, and Dijon mustard. Pour in cashew cream and simmer 3-4 minutes until thickened.
Toss pasta with the stroganoff sauce. Serve garnished with fresh parsley and cracked black pepper.

vgn_edamame_grain_bowl.name: Edamame Grain Bowl with Miso Dressing
vgn_edamame_grain_bowl.description: Warm farro topped with edamame, roasted beets, avocado, pickled radish, and a white miso-ginger dressing
ingredients: 3 radishes, thinly sliced and quick-pickled in rice vinegar
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Toss beet cubes with olive oil and salt. Roast for 20 minutes until tender.
Cook edamame according to package directions (boil or steam 3-4 minutes).
Quick-pickle radish slices in rice vinegar with a pinch of salt for at least 10 minutes.
Whisk miso paste, ginger, rice vinegar, and sesame oil together for the dressing.
Assemble bowl with farro, top with edamame, roasted beets, avocado, pickled radish, and drizzle with miso-ginger dressing. Sprinkle with sesame seeds.

vgn_energy_balls.name: Peanut Butter Chocolate Energy Balls
vgn_energy_balls.description: No-bake energy balls packed with oats, peanut butter, dark chocolate chips, flaxseed, and a touch of maple
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a bowl and mix until a sticky dough forms.
Refrigerate for 15-20 minutes to make the mixture easier to handle.
Roll into 10 balls (about 1 tablespoon each).
Store in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 1 week.

vgn_roasted_chickpeas.name: Spiced Roasted Chickpeas
vgn_roasted_chickpeas.description: Crunchy oven-roasted chickpeas seasoned with smoked paprika, garlic powder, and a hint of cayenne
ingredients: 1 can (400g) chickpeas, drained, rinsed, and patted very dry
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Pat chickpeas very dry with a kitchen towel — this is key for crispiness.
Toss chickpeas with olive oil, smoked paprika, garlic powder, cayenne, and salt.
Spread in a single layer on a baking sheet. Roast for 25-30 minutes, shaking the pan halfway, until golden and crunchy.
Let cool on the baking sheet for 5 minutes — they will crisp up further as they cool.

vgn_hummus_veggie_plate.name: Hummus and Crudité Plate
vgn_hummus_veggie_plate.description: Creamy homemade hummus served with crisp carrot sticks, cucumber, bell pepper, cherry tomatoes, and pita chips
ingredients: Sprinkle of paprika and olive oil on hummus
instructions: Spoon hummus into a small bowl or onto a plate. Drizzle with olive oil and sprinkle with paprika.
Wash and cut carrot, cucumber, and bell pepper into sticks.
Arrange vegetables and pita chips around the hummus for dipping.
Serve immediately.

vgn_trail_mix.name: Tropical Trail Mix
vgn_trail_mix.description: Custom blend of roasted almonds, cashews, pumpkin seeds, dried mango, coconut chips, and dark chocolate chunks
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a small bowl or jar.
Toss gently to distribute evenly.
Store in an airtight container for up to 2 weeks. Perfect for on-the-go snacking.

vgn_guacamole_chips.name: Fresh Guacamole with Tortilla Chips
vgn_guacamole_chips.description: Chunky guacamole made with ripe avocados, fresh lime, cilantro, tomato, and jalapeño, served with crispy corn chips
instructions: Halve and pit the avocado. Scoop into a bowl and mash with a fork, leaving some chunks.
Add lime juice, tomato, red onion, cilantro, jalapeño, and salt. Stir gently to combine.
Taste and adjust salt and lime. Serve immediately with tortilla chips.

vgn_apple_almond_butter.name: Apple Slices with Almond Butter
vgn_apple_almond_butter.description: Crisp apple slices with creamy almond butter, drizzled with cinnamon and topped with hemp seeds
ingredients: 1 large apple (Honeycrisp or Fuji), cored and sliced
instructions: Core and slice the apple into wedges.
Arrange on a plate and serve with almond butter for dipping.
Sprinkle hemp seeds and cinnamon over the almond butter.

vgn_edamame_snack.name: Steamed Edamame with Sea Salt
vgn_edamame_snack.description: Perfectly steamed edamame pods sprinkled with flaky sea salt and a squeeze of fresh lemon
instructions: Bring a pot of water to a boil. Add edamame and cook for 4-5 minutes until tender.
Drain and transfer to a bowl.
Sprinkle with flaky sea salt and a squeeze of lemon. Eat by popping beans from pods.

vgn_banana_nice_cream.name: Chocolate Banana Nice Cream
vgn_banana_nice_cream.description: Creamy frozen banana soft-serve blended with cocoa powder, a swirl of peanut butter, and cacao nibs
instructions: Add frozen banana slices, cocoa powder, and almond milk to a food processor or high-speed blender.
Blend until thick and creamy, scraping down sides as needed. It should have the consistency of soft-serve ice cream.
Scoop into a bowl, swirl in peanut butter, and top with cacao nibs. Serve immediately.

vgn_date_walnut_bites.name: Stuffed Medjool Dates with Walnuts
vgn_date_walnut_bites.description: Plump Medjool dates stuffed with walnut halves and drizzled with a thin layer of melted dark chocolate
instructions: Slit each date open lengthwise and remove the pit.
Press a walnut half into each date.
Melt dark chocolate in a small bowl (microwave in 15-second intervals or use a double boiler).
Drizzle melted chocolate over the stuffed dates and sprinkle with flaky sea salt. Let set for 10 minutes before eating.

vgn_tofu_lettuce_wraps.name: Asian Tofu Lettuce Wraps
vgn_tofu_lettuce_wraps.description: Crispy crumbled tofu with water chestnuts, ginger, and hoisin sauce served in crisp butter lettuce cups
instructions: Heat vegetable oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add crumbled tofu and cook 5-6 minutes until golden and slightly crispy.
Add garlic, ginger, and water chestnuts. Cook 2 minutes.
Stir in hoisin sauce, soy sauce, and sesame oil. Cook 1 more minute until glazed.
Spoon mixture into butter lettuce cups. Top with green onions and a drizzle of sriracha.

vgn_tempeh_chili.name: Smoky Tempeh Chili
vgn_tempeh_chili.description: Hearty three-bean chili with crumbled tempeh, fire-roasted tomatoes, chipotle peppers, and warm spices
ingredients: Fresh cilantro and avocado for garnish
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot. Sauté onion for 5 minutes, add garlic and crumbled tempeh. Cook 5 minutes until tempeh is lightly browned.
Add chili powder, cumin, smoked paprika, and chopped chipotle pepper. Stir for 1 minute.
Pour in fire-roasted tomatoes, all three beans, and vegetable broth. Stir to combine.
Bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer. Cook for 25-30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until thick and flavorful.
Season with salt. Serve topped with fresh cilantro and sliced avocado.

vgn_pesto_pasta.name: Basil Pesto Pasta with Cherry Tomatoes
vgn_pesto_pasta.description: Al dente penne tossed in a vibrant walnut-basil pesto with burst cherry tomatoes and toasted pine nuts
instructions: Cook penne according to package directions until al dente. Reserve 1/4 cup pasta water before draining.
Make pesto: blend basil, walnuts, nutritional yeast, garlic, olive oil, lemon juice, salt, and pepper until smooth.
In a large pan, sauté cherry tomatoes for 3-4 minutes until they start to burst.
Add drained pasta and pesto to the pan. Toss to combine, adding pasta water as needed for a silky consistency.
Serve topped with toasted pine nuts and extra nutritional yeast.

vgn_shepherd_pie.name: Lentil Shepherd's Pie
vgn_shepherd_pie.description: Savory lentil and vegetable filling topped with creamy mashed potatoes and baked until golden
instructions: Boil potatoes until tender, about 15 minutes. Drain and mash with plant butter, oat milk, salt, and pepper until smooth.
Heat olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet. Sauté onion, carrot, and celery for 5 minutes.
Add lentils, frozen peas, tomato paste, vegetable broth, thyme, and rosemary. Simmer 10 minutes until thick.
Spread mashed potatoes evenly over the lentil filling. Score the top with a fork for texture.
Broil for 5-7 minutes until potato topping is golden brown. Let cool 5 minutes before serving.

vgn_coconut_chickpea_stew.name: Coconut Chickpea Spinach Stew
vgn_coconut_chickpea_stew.description: One-pot stew with chickpeas, tomatoes, and spinach in a spiced coconut broth with warm naan
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot. Sauté onion for 4 minutes, add garlic and ginger, cook 1 minute.
Add cumin, coriander, and turmeric. Stir for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Pour in diced tomatoes, coconut milk, and chickpeas. Bring to a simmer and cook for 18-20 minutes.
Stir in spinach and cook until wilted, about 2 minutes. Season with salt and pepper.
Serve in bowls with warm naan and garnish with fresh cilantro.

vgn_tempeh_bibimbap.name: Tempeh Bibimbap Bowl
vgn_tempeh_bibimbap.description: Korean-inspired bowl with marinated tempeh, sautéed vegetables, kimchi, and spicy gochujang sauce over rice
instructions: Marinate tempeh in soy sauce and 1 tsp sesame oil for 10 minutes. Pan-fry in vegetable oil until crispy, 3-4 minutes per side.
Quickly sauté spinach with a drizzle of sesame oil until wilted. Blanch bean sprouts for 1 minute. Julienne carrots and lightly sauté.
Make bibimbap sauce: mix gochujang with maple syrup, 1 tsp sesame oil, and 1 tbsp water.
Arrange rice in a bowl. Top with tempeh, spinach, carrots, bean sprouts, and kimchi in sections.
Drizzle with gochujang sauce, sprinkle with sesame seeds and green onion. Mix everything together before eating.
